title: ranunculus sceleratus subsp. multifidus
intro: Ranunculus sceleratus subsp. multifidus, also known as buttercup or lesser celandine, is a flowering perennial plant native to Europe, Asia, and North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include being anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making it a potential treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of alkaloids and glycosides. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus sceleratus subsp. multifidus is often used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
